summary: Stub package on a name that resembles the LiveKit Agents project
details: Package published as `livekit-agents` contains only a trivial `greet(name)` function returning a hello string. There are no install lifecycle hooks, no network I/O, no shell execution, and no credential or filesystem access. The shipped README is for an unrelated project (`prek`), and the package's advertised description (`A modern npm package`) does not match either the name or the README content. The name overlaps with the well-known LiveKit Agents project namespace, creating a confusion/squat risk for developers who install this expecting that project. No installer-side harm is present in this version's code; the concern is the name-confusion surface and the mismatched README, which warrant human review to confirm legitimate ownership or intent.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
